LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ALBERT CHOW AND DANIEL BURNHAM COURT SURGERY CENTER'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS MOOT. FURTHERMORE, THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED AS TO PORTIONS ALLEGING THE NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS OF PLAINTIFF GLADSHTEYN: (1) PARAGRAPH 41; (2) PARAGRAPH 42, LINES 4-5: "'HAS ALSO SUFFERED EXTREME EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DUE TO THE POTENTIAL BREAK-UP OF HIS MARRIAGE, WHICH WAS PREVIOUSLY A HAPPY ONE. FOLLOWING THE INJURIES, HE'"; (3) PARAGRAPH 43, LINES 11-12: "'AND HE EXPECTS TO CONTINUE TO SUFFER FROM EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IN THE FUTURE"; AND (4) PARAGRAPH 89. THE MOTION TO STRIKE AS IRRELEVANT IS GRANTED AS TO PORTIONS REFERRING TO THE CARE OF PLAINTIFFS DAUGHTER: (5) PARAGRAPH 45; (6) PARAGRAPH 56; AND (7) PARAGRAPH 61. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE REMAINING PORTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complt
Off Calendar Mar-06-2014 Text Ruling

Plaintiffs Rada Katz & Oleg Gladshteyn'S Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-13-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Shyamala T. Rajender, Esq. of the Law Offices of Waukeen McCoy, Tel. 415.675.7705, for plaintiffs and moving party;
John J. Ring, Esq. of Lynch Gilardi & Grummer, Tel. 415.397.2800, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS RADA KATZ & OLEG GLADSHTEYN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied. First, the Proposed Second Amended Complaint seeks to add causes of action for intentional and negligent emotional distress. On October 29, 2012, the Court sustained without leave to amend a demurrer on these same causes of action. Second, the motion does not conform to the Rules of Court. CRC 3.1324, titled "Amended pleadings and amendments to pleadings," applies to this motion. Plaintiffs have not stated what allegations are proposed to be added to the previous pleading, if any, and where by page, paragraph, and line number, the additional allegations are located. (CRC 2.1324(a)(3).) Plaintiffs also have not filed a supporting declaration specifying the effect of the amendment, why the amendment is necessary and proper, when the facts giving rise to the amended allegations were discovered, and the reasons why the request for amendment was not made earlier. (CRC 2.1324(b).) Third, Plaintiffs have not established they acted diligently in seeking amendment. Defendants have submitted evidence that billing information and informed consent agreements were produced pursuant to subpoena in 2011. (Declaration of John Ring, 3; Supplemental Declaration of John Ring, 8.) Plaintiffs' only explanation for not seeking to amend sooner is issues with the expert originally obtained in this action. Plaintiffs offer no explanation of why a different or additional expert was not obtained sooner. Further, Plaintiffs moved to augment their expert witness list in November 2013 to include their new expert witness and offer no explanation why they waited over three months to move to amend their complaint. Fourth, Defendants have demonstrated they will be unduly prejudiced by these amendments. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ALBERT CHOW and DANIEL BURNHAM COURT SURGERY CENTER's Motion To Quash Business Records Subpoena Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1987.1. is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The subject matter of the subpoena duces tecum is relevant to this action as it pertains to surgeries on plaintiff's breast and abdomen. The subject matter of the subpoena is not, however, relevant to this action to the extent it calls for records pertaining to plaintiff's nose or any other body part. No such records pertaining to plaintiff's nose or other body parts shall be produced. To the extent that records contain information pertaining to body parts other than the breast or abdomen, those portions must be redacted prior to production. Within 10 days of this order, United Healthcare Services, Inc. shall produce all relevant documents for the period from January 1, 2009 through December 31, 2011, as described above. The subpoena itself does not call for the production of any privileged documents. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/EHG
